title: Enterosora trichosora
taxonomy: Enterosora trichosora is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This family is commonly known as the polypody ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is part of the larger group Polypodiales, which is a suborder of leptosporangiate ferns. The order Polypodiales is a subgroup of the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is a subgroup of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns.

intro: Enterosora trichosora, also known as Trichomanes trichosorum, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms and promote healing. The bioactive constituents of Enterosora trichosora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Enterosora trichosora is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
